title: Model Selection
description: Test your knowledge of choosing the right AI model for a task

questions.question: When is a smaller, faster model usually the better choice over a larger one?
options: For high-volume, latency-sensitive tasks with simple requirements
Only when no larger model is available
For the most complex reasoning problems
Never — larger is always better
questions.explanation: Smaller models cost less and respond faster, making them a strong fit for high-throughput, latency-sensitive tasks that don't need deep reasoning.

questions.question: What does a model's 'context window' limit?
options: How many users can call it at once
The total tokens of input plus output it can handle in one request
How many languages it understands
The number of parameters it was trained with
questions.explanation: The context window caps how much text — prompt plus generated output — the model can process in a single request.

questions.question: Which factor is most relevant when a task requires up-to-date factual knowledge?
options: The model's training-data cutoff date, or whether it can retrieve external data
The colour of the API dashboard
The model's maximum temperature setting
The programming language of the client
questions.explanation: A model only knows what it was trained on up to its cutoff; for fresh facts you need a recent model or a retrieval/tool step that supplies current data.

questions.question: Why might you evaluate several models on your own test set before committing?
options: Benchmarks rarely reflect performance on your specific task and data
It is required by law
It permanently fine-tunes the models
It lowers the price of every model
questions.explanation: Public benchmarks are generic; measuring candidates against your own representative examples reveals which one actually performs best for your use case.

questions.question: What is a common trade-off when moving to a more capable model?
options: Higher accuracy but greater cost and latency
Lower accuracy but higher cost
No change to cost or speed
Smaller context windows
questions.explanation: More capable models typically deliver better quality but cost more per token and respond more slowly, so the choice balances quality against budget and speed.
